title: actinidia fulvicoma var. lanata
intro: Actinidia fulvicoma var. lanata, also known as the hairy kiwifruit or hairy Chinese vine, is a species of flowering plant in the Actinidiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The hairy kiwifruit can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
